nativeRange.habitat: Seasonal marshes, rice paddies, and muddy stream margins of the Western Ghats and Deccan plateau of southwestern India. The species is primarily a marginal/terrestrial plant during the wet season that has been adapted to submerged aquarium culture.

decorPreferences: background or rear-midground placement in tight bushy groups
medium-to-high PAR lighting to maintain compact form
regular trimming to promote bushy growth and lateral branching

summary: Pogostemon Erectus is an Indian stem plant in the mint family, grown for its dense, conifer-like spires of narrow, needle-shaped bright-green leaves. Widely considered a striking background accent, it is best suited to experienced aquarists who can provide strong light, CO2, and regular trimming.
careNotes:
Pogostemon Erectus is an Indian stem plant in the mint family (Lamiaceae) well known in the aquascaping hobby for its distinctive conifer-like vertical spires of narrow, needle-shaped bright-green leaves whorled tightly around a central stem. It is one of the more recognizable Pogostemon species in the hobby and forms a strong vertical architectural element when planted in dense groups as a background or rear-midground feature.

A taxonomic note that matters in trade: the plant universally sold as 'Pogostemon erectus' since the mid-2000s is now widely accepted to actually be Pogostemon deccanensis (Panigrahi) Press. The true P. erectus, as originally described by Dalzell, is a marginal and terrestrial plant that does not adapt well to submerged aquarium culture. The trade name 'P. erectus' has remained the universal label in the hobby regardless, and Pogostemon deccanensis is listed as an alternate name. Native range is southwestern India — specifically the Western Ghats and Deccan plateau — where it grows in seasonal marshes, rice paddies, and muddy stream margins.

Care is generally rated medium to advanced, depending on the source; it is best approached as a demanding species that requires strong light, CO2 injection, and a nutrient-rich substrate to stay compact and avoid lower-leaf deterioration. Height typically reaches 15-40 cm — substantially smaller than the related P. stellatus — and the plant tends to stay denser and more compact under stronger light. A comfortable water temperature of 22-28 °C, pH 6.0-7.5, and soft-to-moderately-hard water (gH 2-15, kH 1-8) suits it well.

Frequent pruning is required to maintain the bushy form; without it, lower stems tend to shed leaves and the plant's structured appearance breaks down. For best visual impact, it is generally presented in tight groups of multiple stems rather than as a solitary specimen. Unlike its red-pigmented relative P. stellatus, Pogostemon Erectus remains a vibrant green under all conditions and is grown primarily for form and texture rather than color.
breedingNotes: Propagated by stem cuttings. Floating cuttings root readily before being planted.

primary.name: Tropica
primary.notes: Used for species identity, the P. erectus = P. deccanensis taxonomic clarification, native range (Asia, specifically India), height 15-40 cm, leaf width 1-3 cm, family Lamiaceae, medium light demand, CO2 range 6-14 mg/L, moderate growth rate, background placement, medium care difficulty, propagation by stem cuttings, and the 'intense light keeps it compact' note. Primary parameter source.

additional.name: Buce Plant
additional.notes: Confirms family Lamiaceae, Asian origin (cites Thailand — likely incorrect; conflicts with Tropica/Flowgrow which both cite southwestern India and which are followed here), medium-to-high lighting, CO2 recommended, pH 6.0-7.5, moderate growth, background placement, medium care level, distinctive 'pointy thin leaves growing from the center stem,' and propagation by cutting new growth with roots or trimming stems anywhere (producing two shoots at the cut site).

additional.name: Flowgrow Aquatic Plant Database
additional.notes: Confirms family Lamiaceae, native range as southwestern India, synonyms Dysophylla erecta Dalzell and Eusteralis erecta (Dalzell) Panigrahi, and most importantly documents the taxonomic clarification: the plant sold in the hobby as 'Pogostemon erectus' is actually P. deccanensis, while the true Dalzell P. erectus is a marginal/terrestrial plant unsuitable for submerged aquarium culture. The hobby retains the 'erectus' trade name.

additional.name: Wikipedia (Pogostemon)
additional.notes: Confirms genus Pogostemon in family Lamiaceae, native to warmer parts of Asia, Africa, and Australia, and that several Pogostemon species (including P. erectus and P. deccanensis) are grown ornamentally in the aquascaping hobby.
